title: GitHub Copilot CLI Gets Tabs and No-Config-File Tool Setup in Redesigned Terminal UI
gist: GitHub made its redesigned Copilot CLI terminal interface generally available, adding a tabbed layout for issues/PRs/gists, in-session MCP and tool configuration without editing config files, and accessibility features like colorblind themes and auto screen-reader support.

insights.claim: GitHub made the redesigned Copilot CLI terminal interface generally available after previewing it at Microsoft Build 2026, adding tabs for Session, Gists, and repo-scoped Issues and Pull requests.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: Signals GitHub is investing in the CLI as a primary agentic-coding surface, competing with terminal AI tools.
insights.locator: GitHub has made the redesigned terminal interface

insights.claim: Developers can drop an issue or pull request reference into the Copilot prompt with a keystroke to have it investigate, fix, comment on, or review the item without leaving the terminal.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: Reduces context-switching in agentic coding — a stickiness feature that deepens GitHub lock-in.
insights.locator: An interactive Copilot CLI session now presents tabs

insights.claim: Configuring Copilot CLI tools is now a guided in-session experience: /mcp add fills an interactive form and the experimental /mcp search browses the GitHub MCP Registry to install a Model Context Protocol server directly, available immediately without restart.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: Lowering MCP-server install friction accelerates the MCP ecosystem GitHub is trying to anchor.
insights.locator: Configuring the tools that extend Copilot CLI

insights.claim: Copilot CLI adds /skills to toggle skills, /plugin to install plugins from a marketplace, repository, or local path, and /settings for inline configuration.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: A plugin/marketplace surface inside the CLI is an emerging distribution channel worth watching.
insights.locator: Configuring the tools that extend Copilot CLI

insights.claim: The redesigned interface targets accessibility with theme-aware semantic colours, a /theme command offering default, dim, high-contrast, and colorblind modes, and automatic screen-reader support with labelled icons and disabled animations.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: Accessibility investment broadens the addressable developer base and pre-empts enterprise procurement requirements.
insights.locator: The new interface also targets accessibility

insights.claim: GitHub Copilot CLI itself reached general availability in February 2026, and the new UI builds on that with browse-and-act capabilities for issues, PRs, and gists.
insights.speaker: Mark Silvester
insights.so_what: Establishes the release cadence: GA CLI in Feb, UI overhaul by July — rapid iteration on the terminal agent.
insights.locator: The release builds on GitHub Copilot CLI itself reaching general availability
